fault_hypothesis: The overlap detection on line 67 is incorrect because it uses non-strict inequalities (`<=`, `>=`). This allows intervals that are adjacent (touching at a boundary) to be scheduled, whereas the specification requires them to be rejected as conflicts.

spec.expr: (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e) == (end__1 < s or start__1 > e)

constraints.intent: The buggy non-overlap check should be equivalent to a strict non-overlap check.
The new interval must not touch or overlap with any existing slot checked so far.
The start of an interval must be strictly less than its end.

spec.expr: __cbfl_result[0] == False or all(interval[1] < s or interval[0] > e for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: If an interval is accepted, it must not overlap (including touching) any of the originally existing slots.
